commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This needs maintainer API/security judgment rather than an automated repair because the main blocker is choosing the setup-hook owner boundary and credential contract.; Needs attention: The diff adds a new opt-in command-execution and env-forwarding surface for downloaded or installed skills, so the security boundary needs maintainer approval before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve configured skill env f... (sha=a09588a289ba549ae3ab4961de56eb8ab0e0c6e2)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` parsing plus an explicit `allowSetupHooks` / `--allow-setup-hooks` opt-in across skill install/update lifecycle, gateway protocol handlers, CLI commands, docs, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +337, Tests +761, Docs +39. Total +1137 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a new feature PR. The changed code path is source-reviewable, and the PR body supplies local gateway/CLI/lifecycle proof for default-skip and explicit opt-in behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs maintainer API/security judgment rather than an automated repair because the main blocker is choosing the setup-hook owner boundary and credential contract.

target.title: fix #80213: [Feature]: Skill author-defined setup hook (run skill-supplied script on install/update)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80213.

- Keep skill-defined setup hooks disabled by default for ClawHub, upload, source, and gateway installer flows.
- Add an explicit `allowSetupHooks` / `--allow-setup-hooks` opt-in through the gateway protocol, gateway handlers, CLI, and install/update lifecycle paths.
- Document setup hook trust, timeout, failure, env forwarding, and staging semantics, with regression coverage for default-skip and explicit opt-in behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Skill packages can declare `metadata.openclaw.setup.script`, but OpenClaw no longer runs downloaded or installed skill setup hooks by default. Trusted admin/gateway/CLI callers must explicitly opt in, and the opt-in path preserves env sanitization and setup script confinement.
- **Real environment tested:** Existing PR repair worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-80266`; Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64; Node 22.22.0; repository pnpm/tooling; evidence under `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the gateway proof script with `pnpm exec node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof.ts"`; ran targeted lifecycle, CLI, gateway, and frontmatter Vitest suites with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; ran changed-file format checks, docs format/lint, core oxlint shards, core prod/test `run-tsgo` checks, and `git diff --check`.
- **Evidence after fix:** `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof-result.json`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/vitest-setup-hook-opt-in-expanded-verbose-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/vitest-setup-hook-opt-in-cli-gateway-verbose-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/vitest-gateway-skills-clawhub-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/lint-core-setup-hook-opt-in-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/lint-docs-setup-hook-opt-in-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/format-check-changed-ts-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/format-docs-check-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/format-skills-clawhub-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/git-diff-check-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/tsgo-core-prod-setup-hook-opt-in-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/tsgo-core-test-setup-hook-opt-in-fresh.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/verification-summary.md`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway proof reports `defaultGateSkippedHook: true`, `explicitOptInHookRan: true`, `hookKind: "install"`, `allowedEnvForwarded: true`, `gatewayTokenBlocked: true`, and `nodeOptionsBlocked: true`. Lifecycle/setup Vitest passed 5 files / 75 tests; CLI/gateway/frontmatter Vitest passed 6 files / 108 tests; current-head ClawHub gateway handler Vitest passed 2 files / 22 tests; changed-file formatting, docs formatting, core oxlint, markdownlint, core prod/test type checks, and `git diff --check` all passed.
- **What was not tested:** A live published ClawHub skill containing setup metadata was not installed from the public registry. This repair uses the real local gateway upload install path plus lifecycle/CLI/gateway handler tests, and it keeps live ClawHub setup execution behind explicit opt-in rather than default autorun.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high for updating this existing PR. The code path that previously changed the trust boundary is now explicitly gated, the public gateway/CLI opt-in is wired, and local gateway/lifecycle/CLI evidence proves default-skip and opt-in behavior. Confidence is not marked high only because a live published ClawHub registry install was not available in this environment.
- **Patch quality notes:** The warnings around timeout/fallback/catch handling are part of the new setup-hook execution boundary: setup scripts are external process/file inputs, so the runner must report parse/read/execute failures, cap timeouts, and support `sh` for non-executable shell scripts. These are not used to mask the trust-boundary issue; the root behavior change is the explicit opt-in gate across ClawHub, upload, source, gateway, and CLI entry points. The test-only type narrowing in ClawHub assertions checks whether the install transaction received an `afterInstall` callback without changing production types.
- **Target test file:** `src/skills/lifecycle/setup.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/archive-install.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/install.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/source-install.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`, `src/skills/loading/frontmatter.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`, `src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm exec node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof.ts"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts --reporter verbose src/skills/lifecycle/source-install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/setup.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/archive-install.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --reporter verbose src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/skills/loading/frontmatter.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/skills/lifecycle/upload-install.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --split-core`
- `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --reporter verbose src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The original setup-hook implementation exposed a new executable skill metadata surface while enabling the ClawHub install/update path to run downloaded setup scripts by default. At the same time, the public gateway/CLI contract did not consistently expose one deliberate trust-boundary opt-in for the workflow described by the issue, so the risky default path and the intended admin-controlled path diverged.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch makes the trust decision explicit and consistent at the source of the behavior. Setup hooks remain parsed and executable only after the caller opts in through `allowSetupHooks` or `--allow-setup-hooks`; that same decision is propagated through gateway protocol validation, gateway handlers, CLI commands, ClawHub install/update, upload installs, source installs, and local installer execution. The added docs and tests cover both sides of the invariant: default installs/updates do not run setup hooks, and trusted opt-in installs/updates do run them with the existing env sanitizer and confinement checks.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
